description:
You are a data-scientist who has just run an automatic data-cleaning script.  
    The script has already produced a log file at  

        /home/user/datasets/logs/cleaning.log  

    Your job is to analyse that log and generate a one-page, machine-readable
    summary of the cleaning results.

    1. Read the existing log file and extract the following six integer values.  
       • the number of rows originally loaded into memory  
       • the number of rows remaining after the cleaning finished  
       • the number of rows removed because they contained missing values  
       • the number of rows removed because their values were out-of-range  
       • the number of rows removed because they were duplicates  
       • the total number of rows removed (the previous three counts added
         together)

    2. Create the directory  

        /home/user/datasets/reports  

       if it does not yet exist.

    3. Inside that directory create a new file named  

        /home/user/datasets/reports/cleaning_summary.log  

       containing exactly six lines in the **exact order** shown below.
       Every line must follow the pattern KEY=INTEGER with **no** leading or
       trailing spaces and only a single newline character (ASCII 0x0A) after
       the last line:

            LOADED=<integer>
            REMAINING=<integer>
            MISSING=<integer>
            OUT_OF_RANGE=<integer>
            DUPLICATE=<integer>
            REMOVED_TOTAL=<integer>

       For example, if 1 000 rows were loaded and 20 rows were removed because
       they had missing values, the first two lines would look like  

            LOADED=1000
            REMAINING=980

    4. Make sure that the file permissions remain at the default
       user-writable level (no sudo or root actions required).

    5. When everything is finished, you may print the contents of
       /home/user/datasets/reports/cleaning_summary.log to the terminal so a
       human observer can quickly confirm the summary, but the **automated
       tests will verify the file directly**, checking:

         • that /home/user/datasets/reports exists  
         • that cleaning_summary.log exists inside it  
         • that the file contains exactly six newline-separated lines, in the
           order and format specified above, with the correct integer values.

    Hint: you should be able to perform the extraction, directory creation and
    file writing with a short sequence of shell commands that use standard
    utilities such as grep/awk/cut, mkdir, and echo/redirection.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing file that is already present in the container when the
        agent starts.
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/datasets/logs/cleaning.log"><![CDATA[
2023-08-14 09:00:00 INFO Cleaning script initiated
2023-08-14 09:00:01 INFO Loaded 4789 rows
2023-08-14 09:00:02 WARN Removed 123 rows with missing values
2023-08-14 09:00:03 WARN Removed 56 rows with out-of-range values
2023-08-14 09:00:04 WARN Removed 12 duplicate rows
2023-08-14 09:00:05 INFO Finished cleaning successfully: 4598 rows remaining
]]></file>

    <!--
        Expected artefact after the agent completes the task.
    -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/datasets/reports/cleaning_summary.log"><![CDATA[
LOADED=4789
REMAINING=4598
MISSING=123
OUT_OF_RANGE=56
DUPLICATE=12
REMOVED_TOTAL=191
]]></expected_file>
